description:
A commercial manufacturing facility wants to lower its electricity bill using a battery for peak shaving. The facility operates a heavy manufacturing shift from 6:00 AM to 6:00 PM (06:00 to 17:59), creating a peak demand of 250 kW. The baseline load during all other hours (midnight to 05:59, and 18:00 to 23:59) is 50 kW. This schedule runs 365 days a year.

Model a Standalone Battery (Commercial) system using SAM or PySAM to determine the new peak grid demand after battery operation. Use a 200 kW power capacity, 400 kWh energy capacity (2-hour duration) Lithium-ion battery with an Automated Peak Shaving dispatch strategy. Synthesize the 8760-hour diurnal load profile based on the facility's schedule (total annual load 912,500 kWh).

Save the results to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/peak_shaving_results.json with the following keys:
- battery_power_kw
- battery_energy_kwh
- original_peak_demand_kw
- new_peak_demand_kw
- peak_reduction_kw
- annual_load_kwh

Additionally, save your PySAM script to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/commercial_peak_shaving.py or your SAM project file to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/Commercial_Peak_Shaving.sam.
